properties.remark: MESONET STATION DCRM8 DEEP CREEK RAWS.
ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS OF 50 TO 60 MPH. PEA SIZE HAIL FALLING. TREES HAD LEAF DAMAGE.

SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEO AND PHOTOS OF HAIL ALONG THE GALLATIN RIVER. HAIL LASTED SEVERAL MINUTES. MINOR DEFOLIATION OF VEGETATION NOTED IN VIDEO. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR AND SUBJECT TO FINAL ENTRY IN STORM DATA.
VERY LITTLE RAIN WAS FALLING WITH THE HAIL.

Spotter reports various sizes of hail fell for nearly 20 minutes. Largest was ping pong size. Time of report is when largest hail size correlates to radar data. Estimated peak wind gusts of around 50 mph. Impacts from hail noted to hay crop.
Quarter size hail reported by spotter along with 0.62 of rain in 21 minutes.
Pictures submitted of hail being compared to a quarter. Occurred near Lewistown Pool.

Water and debris rushed across several areas of Wilson Butte Road resulting in damage and impacts. Water flowed out of higher terrain next to the road with water depth being 2 to 3 feet at times, per depth of debris and water line. Water and debris entered at least two homes. One culvert pass was washed out with drainage pipes unearthed. Road was impassable due to continued flow of high water for approx an hour. NWS employee located a half mile away had water enter basement.
Images sent to office of hail compared to coins.
AWOS station KGFA Malmstrom AFB.
Fergus County Sheriffs Office reports portions of Casino Creek Road beginning to wash out from heavy rain. Additional details expected to be included in Storm Data once available.
Fergus County Sheriffs Office reports portions of Cottonwood Creek Road beginning to wash out from heavy rain.
Helena-Lewis and Clark National Forest reports S Fork Teton Road was washed out during a thunderstorm.
Significant debris flow crossed Benchmark Road west of Ford Creek, resulting in road being impassable.
Minor debris flows and rock slides blocking Highway 49 in some areas.
Mesonet station MTGYS Geyser (MT DOT).

Broadwater Co EM reports portions of N Creek Road closed due to high water. Portions of Highway 12 also being impacted from mud and rock slides, possibly as a result of heavy rain at nearby burn scar from summer 2021 fire.

Broadcast media submitted picture of landspout across rural Hill Co. Debris cloud at base visible in photo. Two additional well defined funnel clouds also evident in image. Storm motion was slowly to the WNW.
Meagher Co Sheriff reports that Highway 360 west of White Sulphur Springs was closed due to flooding and water on the road. As of LSR issuance, road has since reopened.

UPDATED REPORT and correction for time of event. NWS conducted storm survey on reported crop damage destruction, but found no evidence of any crop damage occurring. Previous report: From 630 PM to 705 PM, observer reports receiving two inches of rainfall, with lots of dime to mostly quarter size hail, causing a great deal of crop damage and destruction of fields.
From 630 PM to 705 PM, observer reports receiving two inches of rainfall, with lots of dime to mostly quarter size hail, causing a great deal of crop damage and destruction of fields.

Several reports of hail from Facebook, Twitter, and Gallatin County Sheriff, ranging from dime size to golf ball size. Reports also mentioned that hail accumulated to around 2 inches, causing a driver on MT Highway 86 - Bridger Canyon Road - to have to pull off to the side of the road. Report from Facebook also estimated wind gusts of 70 mph, shown in included video.

Report from Facebook also estimated wind gusts of 70 mph, shown in included video.
Second hand report from Facebook of thunderstorm wind and hail damage. Broken windows and siding on a residence with a significant amount of hail.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 18 ESE Maudlow. Facebook report from near the town of Sedan included video that showed damage from hail and strong winds. A plastic circular hanging thermometer had several chunks taken out of it. A window air conditioning unit had several dents in it, and the vertical blinds adjacent to the unit were damaged.

Facebook report included video that showed damage from hail and strong winds. A plastic circular hanging thermometer had several chunks taken out of it. A window air conditioning unit had several dents in it, and the vertical blinds adjacent to the unit were damaged.

Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 18 ESE Maudlow. Facebook report estimated wind gusts of 70 mph, shown in included video, from near the town of Sedan. Wind report estimated because anemometer was broken. Same storm produced several hail and thunderstorm damage reports.

Corrected report to reflect time of actual hail occurrence. Spotter just arrived in town and estimates that the hail ended approximately 10 minutes ago, but there were still a few quarter size hailstones on the ground.
Blaine County Deputy EM reported golf ball size hail at their property.

Started hailing around 905 pm and just ended. Hail is covering the ground. It started as pea to dime size after 5 minutes, but it became larger after then. Most stones on the ground were around quarter size. No damage reported as of now.
Social media videos and photos submitted of a landspout northwest of Lima Reservoir. Rain shower in the vicinity.
A well documented tornado via images from social media as well as the local emergency manager showed a tornado in the Whitetail Basin. No structural damage occurred.
